citations.authors: Heathcote, A., Popiel, S. J., & Mewhort, D. J. K.
citations.title: Analysis of response time distributions: An example using the Stroop task
citations.quote: Ex-Gaussian fits to Stroop color-naming RTs yielded mu around 480-540 ms for congruent/control conditions in young adults

citations.authors: Spieler, D. H., Balota, D. A., & Faust, M. E.
citations.title: Stroop performance in healthy younger and older adults and in individuals with dementia of the Alzheimer's type
citations.quote: Younger adults' ex-Gaussian mu for congruent Stroop trials was approximately 510 ms (vocal response variant comparable in mu to manual)

citations.authors: Steinhauser, M., & Hübner, R.
citations.title: Distinguishing response conflict and task conflict in the Stroop task: Evidence from ex-Gaussian distribution analysis
citations.quote: Ex-Gaussian mu estimates for congruent trials clustered between 490 and 560 ms across blocks

citations.authors: Heathcote, A., Popiel, S. J., & Mewhort, D. J. K.
citations.title: Analysis of response time distributions: An example using the Stroop task
citations.quote: Sigma estimates for color-naming Stroop ranged 40-75 ms across congruent conditions

citations.authors: Spieler, D. H., Balota, D. A., & Faust, M. E.
citations.title: Stroop performance in healthy younger and older adults and in individuals with dementia of the Alzheimer's type
citations.quote: Sigma in younger healthy adults averaged ~55 ms for congruent trials

citations.authors: Heathcote, A., Popiel, S. J., & Mewhort, D. J. K.
citations.title: Analysis of response time distributions: An example using the Stroop task
citations.quote: Tau (exponential) component for congruent Stroop trials clustered between 110 and 180 ms

citations.authors: Spieler, D. H., Balota, D. A., & Faust, M. E.
citations.title: Stroop performance in healthy younger and older adults and in individuals with dementia of the Alzheimer's type
citations.quote: Younger-adult tau on congruent trials was ~130-160 ms

congruent.rationale: Healthy adult congruent-trial RTs in keyboard-response Stroop typically center near 650-700ms total mean. Ex-Gaussian decomposition (Heathcote et al., Spieler et al.) yields a Gaussian component (mu) around 500-550ms with moderate sigma (~50-70ms) capturing perceptual/motor variability, and an exponential tail (tau) around 130-170ms reflecting occasional attentional lapses. Word-reading interference is minimal here, so tau is on the lower end.

citations.authors: Heathcote, A., Popiel, S. J., & Mewhort, D. J. K.
citations.title: Analysis of response time distributions: An example using the Stroop task
citations.quote: Most of the Stroop interference was localized in tau, with mu shifting only 20-50 ms between congruent and incongruent

citations.authors: Spieler, D. H., Balota, D. A., & Faust, M. E.
citations.title: Stroop performance in healthy younger and older adults and in individuals with dementia of the Alzheimer's type
citations.quote: Incongruent mu was approximately 530-570 ms, modestly elevated above congruent mu

citations.authors: Steinhauser, M., & Hübner, R.
citations.title: Distinguishing response conflict and task conflict in the Stroop task: Evidence from ex-Gaussian distribution analysis
citations.quote: Incongruent mu estimates ranged 520-590 ms

citations.authors: Heathcote, A., Popiel, S. J., & Mewhort, D. J. K.
citations.title: Analysis of response time distributions: An example using the Stroop task
citations.quote: Sigma rose modestly from congruent to incongruent (typically +5-15 ms)

citations.authors: Spieler, D. H., Balota, D. A., & Faust, M. E.
citations.title: Stroop performance in healthy younger and older adults and in individuals with dementia of the Alzheimer's type
citations.quote: Incongruent sigma in younger adults averaged ~60-70 ms

citations.authors: Heathcote, A., Popiel, S. J., & Mewhort, D. J. K.
citations.title: Analysis of response time distributions: An example using the Stroop task
citations.quote: The bulk of the Stroop interference effect (~60-100 ms) was carried by an increase in tau on incongruent trials

citations.authors: Spieler, D. H., Balota, D. A., & Faust, M. E.
citations.title: Stroop performance in healthy younger and older adults and in individuals with dementia of the Alzheimer's type
citations.quote: Tau increased by ~60-80 ms from congruent to incongruent in healthy young adults

citations.authors: Steinhauser, M., & Hübner, R.
citations.title: Distinguishing response conflict and task conflict in the Stroop task: Evidence from ex-Gaussian distribution analysis
citations.quote: Incongruent tau ranged 190-260 ms, substantially elevated above congruent

incongruent.rationale: Incongruent RTs are typically ~80-120ms slower than congruent in healthy adults. Ex-Gaussian decompositions consistently localize most of the Stroop effect in the tau parameter (slow tail) with smaller increases in mu and sigma. Setting tau ~215 vs 150 for congruent (+65ms) and a small mu shift (+35ms) produces a ~100ms total Stroop effect consistent with classic published norms.

citations.authors: MacLeod, C. M.
citations.title: Half a century of research on the Stroop effect: An integrative review
citations.quote: Substantial practice-related speeding occurs early in Stroop practice, with the largest gains within the first 10-20 trials and continued more gradual improvement thereafter

citations.authors: Logan, G. D.
citations.title: Toward an instance theory of automatization
citations.quote: Power-law speedup with practice; early-trial RT decrements of 20-50 ms are typical for choice-RT tasks

citations.authors: Logan, G. D.
citations.title: Toward an instance theory of automatization
citations.quote: Power-function fits show the steepest RT decrease across approximately the first 10 trials before approaching asymptote

citations.authors: MacLeod, C. M.
citations.title: Half a century of research on the Stroop effect: An integrative review
citations.quote: Most early-block speedup in Stroop tasks is observed within roughly the first dozen trials of stimulus-response mapping

practice_effect.rationale: With no practice block and only 15 trials, modest within-task speedup is expected. Published Stroop studies show RTs decrease ~20-40ms across the first ~10 trials before stabilizing as participants familiarize with the stimulus-response mapping (r/g/b/y key map).

fatigue_effect.rationale: Fatigue effects in Stroop typically only emerge after several hundred trials or extended sessions. With only 15 trials, no measurable fatigue is expected.

citations.authors: Rabbitt, P. M. A.
citations.title: Errors and error correction in choice-response tasks
citations.quote: Responses immediately following an error were ~30-60 ms slower than mean correct RT in choice tasks

citations.authors: Notebaert, W., Houtman, F., Van Opstal, F., Gevers, W., Fias, W., & Verguts, T.
citations.title: Post-error slowing: An orienting account
citations.quote: Post-error slowing magnitudes of 25-50 ms were observed across choice-RT paradigms including Stroop-like conflict tasks

citations.authors: Danielmeier, C., & Ullsperger, M.
citations.title: Post-error adjustments
citations.quote: Across studies, post-error slowing typically falls in the 20-80 ms range depending on task and response-stimulus interval

post_error_slowing.rationale: Post-error slowing of ~30-60ms is robustly documented in Stroop and other choice-RT tasks (Rabbitt, Laming, Notebaert). However, with 15 trials and ~95-98% accuracy, only ~0-1 errors are expected, so this effect will rarely manifest in any given session.

citations.authors: Gratton, G., Coles, M. G. H., & Donchin, E.
citations.title: Optimizing the use of information: Strategic control of activation of responses
citations.quote: Compatibility (interference) effects were reduced by ~15-30 ms following an incompatible relative to a compatible trial

citations.authors: Egner, T.
citations.title: Congruency sequence effects and cognitive control
citations.quote: The reduction of Stroop/flanker interference following incongruent trials (Gratton effect) is robustly ~10-30 ms

citations.authors: Kerns, J. G., Cohen, J. D., MacDonald, A. W., Cho, R. Y., Stenger, V. A., & Carter, C. S.
citations.title: Anterior cingulate conflict monitoring and adjustments in control
citations.quote: Stroop interference was reduced on iI relative to cI trials by approximately 20 ms

conflict_adaptation.rationale: The Gratton/conflict-adaptation effect (reduced Stroop interference following an incongruent trial) is well-documented in Stroop paradigms. Magnitude is typically 15-30ms reduction in interference on iI vs cI trials. Enabled but small given the short trial count.

time_on_task.rationale: Time-on-task vigilance decrements require sustained performance over many minutes. 15 trials at ~1-2s each = under 60s total, far below the threshold for vigilance decrement.

between_subject_jitter.rationale: Healthy adult Stroop RT means vary across individuals with SD ~80-110ms (rt_mean_sd_ms=90). The Stroop effect itself varies across participants with SD ~30-45ms (rt_condition_sd_ms=35). Ex-Gaussian sigma and tau show multiplicative individual differences, scaled within [0.75, 1.3] of central values. Accuracy is near-ceiling with SD ~2-3% (accuracy_sd=0.025). Omissions are rare and tightly clustered near zero (omission_sd=0.005).

citations.authors: Hedge, C., Powell, G., & Sumner, P.
citations.title: The reliability paradox: Why robust cognitive tasks do not produce reliable individual differences
citations.quote: Between-subject SD of mean RT in the Stroop task was 80-110 ms across two large samples

citations.authors: Spieler, D. H., Balota, D. A., & Faust, M. E.
citations.title: Stroop performance in healthy younger and older adults and in individuals with dementia of the Alzheimer's type
citations.quote: Standard deviation of subject-mean RTs in young healthy adults was approximately 90 ms

citations.authors: Hedge, C., Powell, G., & Sumner, P.
citations.title: The reliability paradox: Why robust cognitive tasks do not produce reliable individual differences
citations.quote: Between-subject SD of the Stroop interference effect (incongruent - congruent RT) was approximately 30-45 ms

citations.authors: MacLeod, C. M.
citations.title: Half a century of research on the Stroop effect: An integrative review
citations.quote: Individual variability in the magnitude of Stroop interference is substantial; SDs of 25-50 ms across studies

citations.authors: Spieler, D. H., Balota, D. A., & Faust, M. E.
citations.title: Stroop performance in healthy younger and older adults and in individuals with dementia of the Alzheimer's type
citations.quote: Subject-level sigma and tau showed roughly multiplicative variation, with individual values approximately 0.7-1.4 times the group central tendency

citations.authors: Heathcote, A., Popiel, S. J., & Mewhort, D. J. K.
citations.title: Analysis of response time distributions: An example using the Stroop task
citations.quote: Subject-level tau spanned roughly 75-135% of the group mean across the studied sample

citations.authors: Hedge, C., Powell, G., & Sumner, P.
citations.title: The reliability paradox: Why robust cognitive tasks do not produce reliable individual differences
citations.quote: Between-subject SD of accuracy in keyboard-response Stroop was approximately 2-4% in young adult samples

citations.authors: MacLeod, C. M.
citations.title: Half a century of research on the Stroop effect: An integrative review
citations.quote: Stroop tasks consistently yield near-ceiling accuracy in healthy adults (>95%), with low between-subject variance

citations.authors: Hedge, C., Powell, G., & Sumner, P.
citations.title: The reliability paradox: Why robust cognitive tasks do not produce reliable individual differences
citations.quote: Omission/non-response rates in self-paced Stroop without deadline are typically <1% with very tight between-subject distribution

citations.authors: MacLeod, C. M.
citations.title: Half a century of research on the Stroop effect: An integrative review
citations.quote: Without a response deadline, omissions are rare in Stroop and clustered near zero across participants

reasoning_chain.inference: Identified paradigm 'Stroop' with 2 stimuli. Source files: jspsych.js, plugin-html-keyboard-response.js, plugin-instructions.js, runner-D7ZihNOY.js, jspsych.css, styles.css, inline_script_1.js, inline_script_2.js.
Produced ex-Gaussian (mu/sigma/tau) parameters for 2 conditions; enabled 3 temporal effects.
Produced 35 citations across 15 numeric parameters with literature_range and between_subject_sd.
Tagged sensitivity on 20 parameters; 5 are high-sensitivity.
